Title: Genetic Determinants and Breeding Strategies for Rice Cooking and Eating Quality: A Comprehensive Review

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes grain protein accumulation and shifts starch composition toward higher amylose and lower amylopectin, generally reducing CEQ while improving nutritional protein quality.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qPC1 encodes OsAAP6 and boosts seed storage protein synthesis by transporting amino acids. 2. It regulates starch genes by promoting GBSS/SS/DBE and inhibiting BE, thereby increasing amylose and decreasing amylopectin. 3. Introgression of a high-expression OsAAP6 allele enhanced rice nutritional quality, whereas knockout of OsAAP6/OsAAP1 reduced protein and improved CEQ.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL/gene cloning; expression analysis; introgression; genome editing/knockout
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because it primarily functions as an amino acid transporter rather than a catalytic enzyme or transcription factor.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for increasing grain nutritional protein content, but should be balanced against eating quality; downregulation/editing can improve CEQ.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher protein may reduce eating quality.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates vesicular trafficking relevant to storage protein accumulation, thereby influencing CEQ indirectly.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The review cites Rab5a-VPS9a vesicular pathway as important for storage protein regulation. 2. Storage proteins, especially glutelin, negatively affect CEQ by inhibiting starch gelatinisation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior functional studies
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Small GTPase pathway and guanine nucleotide exchange factor-related vesicular trafficking module
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a protein trafficking pathway affecting glutelin deposition rather than as pathway enzyme or TF.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate storage protein deposition for CEQ improvement, but detailed deployment is not established.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cutin monomers; grain lipid-related quality traits
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Affects cutin monomer synthesis and grain yield, contributing to lipid-related grain quality.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FGC3 was identified by mGWAS and pGWAS. 2. It can be regulated by OsMADS6/OsMADS17. 3. It affects cutin monomer synthesis and grain yield.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in abstract and lipid section as a CEQ-related lipid metabolism gene, but precise biochemical function is not detailed in this review.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate locus for coordinated grain quality and yield improvement.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression causes oil body swelling and decreased triglyceride levels.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OLE16/OLE18 are involved in oil body organization. 2. Their suppression leads to enlarged oil bodies and reduced triglyceride accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential targets for seed lipid storage engineering, though CEQ consequences require balancing.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing impairs seed germination and reduces fatty acid content.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing of OsLTL36 reduces fatty acid content. 2. It also impairs seed germination.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene silencing; seed phenotype and fatty acid measurement
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be relevant to lipid accumulation, but not an obvious CEQ breeding target due to germination defects.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function disrupts redox balance and reduces fatty acid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLPD1 is required for proper redox balance. 2. Its loss reduces fatty acid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lipid metabolism target but may have broader physiological effects.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redox imbalance and possible growth-related defects.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function alleles permit GABald accumulation and 2AP formation, conferring fragrance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Active BADH2 in non-fragrant rice converts 2AP precursor GABald to GABA. 2. Mutant BADH2 allows GABald accumulation and 2AP formation. 3. Nineteen major BADH2 allelic variants are summarized as determinants of rice fragrance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Allelic analysis; mutation-function relationship; metabolite association
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for fragrant rice breeding via allele selection or genome editing.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases chalkiness, amylose, and gel consistency but decreases protein.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chalk5 overexpression raises chalkiness. 2. It increases amylose and gel consistency. 3. It lowers protein and is implicated in balancing starch and protein accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary locus when selecting for grain appearance and CEQ.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces white-core rate and protein, especially glutelin, while increasing starch, amylose, and eating quality.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WCR1 overexpression reduces white-core rate. 2. It lowers protein, especially glutelin. 3. It increases starch, amylose, and eating quality.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; grain trait and storage compound profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve both appearance and eating quality.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout worsens cooked rice luster and eating quality and alters viscosity; introgression of Nipponbare LCG1 improves these traits.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LCG1 knockout decreases cooked rice luster and CEQ. 2. It alters viscosity properties. 3. Introgression of the Nipponbare LCG1 fragment reduces chalkiness and improves eating quality.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout; introgression; viscosity and eating-quality assessment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful donor locus for improving luster, chalkiness, and CEQ.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of NF-Y regulators to influence sucrose import and endosperm filling, affecting storage accumulation and CEQ.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NF-YC12 interacts with NF-YB1 to regulate OsSUT1. 2. OsSUT family dominates sugar transport related to grain filling and starch deposition. 3. Sugar transport efficiency influences chalkiness and eating quality.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of previous regulatory and functional studies
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to optimize grain filling and reduce chalkiness.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in glucose/galactose transport, influencing grain sugar/starch and eating quality.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSWEET1b participates in glucose/galactose transport. 2. It influences grain sugar/starch composition and eating quality.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior functional analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate sugar transport target for grain filling and CEQ improvement.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of qGT3/OsMADS1 to enhance grain plumpness through sugar transport during grain filling.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qGT3 encodes OsMADS1 and regulates grain thickness partly by modulating MST4. 2. MST4 affects sugar transport during grain filling and thus influences grain quality.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential secondary target for improving grain filling and eating quality.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates glucan chains using ADP-glucose to synthesize amylose in starch granules.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal polymer synthesis step within amylose branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point and central determinant
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen fertilisation level; temperature during grain development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines rice texture, hardness/stickiness balance, CEQ, and consumer preference.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls amylose content and pleiotropically affects protein, lipid, grain transparency, taste value, and overall CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural allele analysis; transgenic allele series; QTL mapping; GWAS; expression/splicing analysis; enzyme activity analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wx is the central hub and most important determinant of rice CEQ through control of amylose synthesis. 2. Multiple alleles modulate GBSSI activity, mRNA level, splicing efficiency, starch-granule binding, and thus amylose content. 3. Wx also pleiotropically regulates amylopectin, storage proteins, starch lipids/non-starch lipids, grain transparency, and taste value. 4. Breeding and editing of Wx are core strategies for CEQ improvement.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: At least nine natural alleles are summarized, including wx, Wxa, Wxb, Wxin, Wxla/Wxmw, Wxmp, Wxmq, Wxop/Wxhp, Wxlv, and the EMS-derived Wxela allele.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primary breeding target for tailoring regional eating preference; useful in marker-assisted selection, allele introgression, CRISPR editing of promoter/exon/intron/uORF, and pyramiding with ALK/GS3.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low-amylose alleles often cause dull/opaque grain appearance unless favorable alleles such as Wxla/Wxmw are used.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous Wx promoter; edited cis-elements/uORF6 in genome-editing applications

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Extends amylopectin branch chains during starch synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate structural component of target polymer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alters amylopectin chain-length distribution and thereby CEQ, with japonica SSI alleles generally associated with better quality than indica alleles.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSI mainly regulates amylopectin synthesis. 2. Its absence or downregulation alters chain-length distribution without major effects on starch granule morphology due to compensation by other SSs. 3. Japonica SSI alleles confer shorter amylopectin chains and better eating quality than indica alleles.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful minor target for optimizing amylopectin fine structure and CEQ.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongates amylopectin chains to produce medium-length branches that strongly affect gelatinisation temperature.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms structural determinant of target starch properties
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for gelatinisation temperature
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch cooking behavior and CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls starch gelatinisation temperature through synthesis of medium amylopectin chains; favorable alleles improve CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALK (OsSSIIa) is the major determinant of starch gelatinisation temperature. 2. Mutants reduce medium chains and increase short chains, lowering GT. 3. Multiple alleles with high- and low-GT effects are summarized. 4. Introgression of the 9311 ALK allele into Nipponbare improved CEQ.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review also states that OsSSIIa mutation can increase amylose, resistant starch, lipids, and amylose-lipid complexes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major complementary target to Wx for CEQ breeding and gene pyramiding.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contributes to amylopectin chain elongation and influences expression of Wx and OsSSIIa.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies structural component of target polymer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation lowers medium chains, increases short chains, and improves rice cooking quality.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Downregulating OsSSIIb reduces Wx and OsSSIIa expression. 2. It decreases DP13-24 chains and increases DP5-12 chains. 3. These changes improve cooking quality. 4. OsSSIIb mutation shows partial redundancy relative to OsSSIIa.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for fine-tuning amylopectin structure and CEQ.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to long-chain amylopectin architecture affecting starch properties.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSIII synthesizes long B-chains of amylopectin. 2. ss3b and ss3a-ss3b mutants alter B1 and B3 chain distribution.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for advanced starch-structure engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: transient starch granule initiation/amylopectin long chains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Initiates transient starch granules; SSIVb elongates long amylopectin chains.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SSIVb elongates long chains but SSIVa/b reportedly do not strongly affect endosperm starch content/structure.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSIV initiates transient starch granules. 2. SSIVb elongates amylopectin long chains. 3. SSIIIa and SSIVb overlap in synthesis of DP≥42 branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary target for starch structural modification.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves α-1,4 bonds and transfers glucan segments to C6 positions, generating α-1,6 branches in amylopectin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts mainly on medium/long B-chains to shape amylopectin architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBEI acts on medium and long B-chains. 2. It contributes to amylopectin branch formation relevant to starch properties.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Generates short amylopectin branches, especially DP6-12 chains.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shapes short-chain amylopectin distribution affecting starch cooking properties.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Isoform functional characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBEIIa produces DP6-12 short chains. 2. SBEII enzymes are more efficient than SBEI in forming short A-chains.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Controls formation of very short amylopectin branch chains in the endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specialized regulator of short-chain amylopectin structure in rice endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Isoform functional characterization summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBEIIb is rice endosperm-specific. 2. It mainly controls DP6-7 short chains.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-1,6 linkages to remove improper branches during amylopectin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ensures correct amylopectin synthesis; ISA1 forms homo- and hetero-oligomers, with the hetero-oligomer dominating in endosperm biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical and oligomeric studies summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBEs including ISA are essential for correct amylopectin synthesis. 2. ISA1 can form homo- and hetero-oligomers. 3. The hetero-oligomer is dominant in rice endosperm amylopectin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-1,6-linked branches and partly compensates for ISA function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions mainly in branch hydrolysis with compensatory effects on ISA.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PUL degrades α-1,6-linked branches. 2. It has a compensatory effect on ISA.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a double bond to convert linoleic acid into α-linolenic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ALA formation and contributes to unsaturated fatty acid composition relevant to CEQ and aroma precursors.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFAD3 catalyzes conversion of linoleic acid to ALA. 2. It is part of rice lipid metabolism impacting CEQ-related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving nutritional and sensory lipid quality.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleic acid to linoleic acid conversion
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates oleic acid to linoleic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with high-oleic target product by consuming oleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppression increases oleic acid and decreases linoleic and palmitic acid; knockout yields high-unsaturated-fatty-acid rice.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression; knockout/genome editing; fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNAi suppression of OsFAD2-1 increases oleic acid content. 2. It decreases linoleic and palmitic acid levels. 3. FAD2-1 knockout is highlighted as a route to modify fatty acid composition for quality improvement.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important genome-editing target for healthier fatty acid composition and CEQ-associated lipid traits.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cloned loci associated with variation in rice fatty acid composition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; linkage analysis; RIL validation; cloning
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Forty-six fatty acid-related loci were identified by GWAS and linkage analysis. 2. Sixteen were validated in RILs. 3. PAL6, LIN6, MYR2, and ARA6 were cloned as fatty acid-related genes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific biochemical assignments are not detailed in this review; retained as a grouped summary exactly as presented.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: grain protein content and taste value
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Modulates expression of OsGluA2, OsAAP6, RM1, and OsGluD
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important recently identified module controlling protein content and taste value
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates rice protein content and taste value by modulating storage-protein related genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Module characterization and target-gene expression analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGATA7 forms a regulatory module with SMOS1. 2. This module regulates rice protein content and taste value. 3. It modulates OsGluA2, OsAAP6, RM1, and OsGluD expression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to balance nutritional protein and CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: grain protein content and taste value
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Acts with OsGATA7 in a regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates with OsGATA7 in modulating protein content and taste value.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Module characterization summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SMOS1 partners with OsGATA7. 2. Together they regulate genes related to storage protein accumulation and CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Balance of starch and storage protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Antagonistic transcriptional regulation via heterodimer with OsNAC42
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Starch/protein genes (not individually enumerated in the review excerpt)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates grain protein content while positively regulating amylose-associated quality pathways
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulatory module balancing quality and yield
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces grain protein content and enhances amylose content and eating quality.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional genetic analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ghd7 negatively regulates rice grain protein content. 2. It positively regulates amylose content and eating quality. 3. It forms a heterodimer with OsNAC42 to antagonistically regulate starch/protein genes and balance quality and yield.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for coordinated improvement of CEQ and yield.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because it balances yield and quality, deployment likely requires allele-specific optimization.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Exerts effects opposite to Ghd7 on starch/protein genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Opposes Ghd7 by increasing grain protein content and reducing amylose/eating quality-related effects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional genetic analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC42 exerts effects opposite to Ghd7 on grain protein content and CEQ. 2. It forms a heterodimer with Ghd7 to antagonistically regulate storage pathways. 3. It is recognized as a key regulatory module in seed storage accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning the starch–protein tradeoff, but overactivity may impair CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms physical interaction with RPBF and heterodimers with RISBZ2-5
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synergistically activates SSP genes and supports accumulation of protein, starch, and lipids.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Physical interaction and downregulation phenotypes summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RISBZ1 and RPBF synergistically activate seed storage protein genes. 2. Their downregulation reduces seed protein, starch, and lipids. 3. RISBZ family members can form heterodimers, broadening regulatory control.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important seed storage regulator, though manipulation may broadly alter grain composition.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-activates SSP genes and supports seed protein, starch, and lipid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Physical interaction and downregulation phenotypes summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPBF acts together with RISBZ1 to activate SSP genes. 2. Downregulation reduces protein, starch, and lipids.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding and heterodimer-mediated transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds motifs in Wx and α-globulin genes, linking starch and protein regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Binding/interaction studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RISBZ2 is part of the RISBZ family. 2. It binds Wx and α-globulin motifs. 3. It likely coordinates starch and storage protein regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SSP/starch gene activation and ER protein processing
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SSP genes; starch genes; OsBIP1; PDIL1-1; chalk-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates storage protein and starch genes and regulates ER protein processing, affecting storage accumulation and chalkiness.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Target regulation and functional studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsbZIP60 activates SSP and starch genes. 2. It regulates ER protein processing via OsBIP1 and PDIL1-1. 3. It also controls chalk-related genes, integrating storage deposition with grain quality.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential integrator target for improving storage deposition and grain appearance.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional redundancy with OsNAC26; regulated by OsNAC25 loops
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates SSP/starch genes; single mutants show minor effects but combined loss with OsNAC26 reduces starch and SSPs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC20 activates SSP and starch genes. 2. It is functionally redundant with OsNAC26. 3. Double mutation strongly reduces starch and storage proteins.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with OsNAC20 to activate SSP/starch genes and support endosperm storage accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and double mutant analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC26 acts redundantly with OsNAC20. 2. Double mutation with OsNAC20 reduces starch and SSP accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Balancing starch gene expression and accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts upstream of OsNAC20/26 to balance starch gene expression and storage accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Regulatory studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC25 is an upstream regulator of OsNAC20/26. 2. It forms positive and negative loops with them to balance starch gene expression and accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sucrose transport and endosperm storage regulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: NF-YC12 interacts with NF-YB1; bHLH144 stabilizes NF-YB1 to activate Wx
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coordinates sucrose transport and storage accumulation by regulating OsSUT1 and promoting Wx activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction and promoter-binding studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NF-YC12 interacts with NF-YB1 to regulate OsSUT1. 2. It binds FLO6 and OsGS1;3 promoters. 3. Through bHLH144-stabilized NF-YB1, the module activates Wx and coordinates starch/protein accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pivotal component of the OsNF-Y complex, which the review identifies as central to storage regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value systems target for coordinating starch, protein, sugar transport, and temperature responses.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms NF-Y complexes; stabilized by bHLH144
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key NF-Y component mediating sucrose transport regulation and Wx activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction and promoter studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NF-YB1 partners with NF-YC12. 2. It regulates OsSUT1 and contributes to activation of Wx. 3. Its stabilization by bHLH144 supports storage regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein stabilization and transcriptional activation support
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilizes NF-YB1 to activate Wx and support endosperm storage regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/regulatory studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH144 interacts with the NF-Y module. 2. It stabilizes NF-YB1 to activate Wx.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Grain filling, sugar transport, and storage balance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of MST4 and suppression of Wx
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces amylose and protein while increasing total starch, amylopectin, grain plumpness, and eating quality.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional allele analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qGT3 encodes OsMADS1. 2. OsMADS1nyz activates MST4 to enhance grain plumpness. 3. It suppresses Wx, reducing amylose and protein while increasing total starch, amylopectin, and CEQ. 4. Other OsMADS1 mutations disrupt starch gene expression and impair eating quality.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important node for simultaneous improvement of grain shape/filling and CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsNF-YB9; OsNF-YC8; OsNF-YC9; OsNF-YC10; OsNF-YC11; OsNF-YC12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates Wx and PDIL1-1 to control starch and protein accumulation; loss reduces starch/amylose and raises protein.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Complex and functional studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSPL14/IPA1 regulates endosperm starch and protein accumulation. 2. It acts with OsNF-YB9/YC8-12 in a complex. 3. Loss of function reduces starch/amylose and increases protein.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for balancing yield architecture traits with grain quality.

Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsSPL14/IPA1; OsNF-YC8; OsNF-YC9; OsNF-YC10; OsNF-YC11; OsNF-YC12
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to complex control of starch and protein accumulation via Wx and PDIL1-1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Complex studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNF-YB9 works with OsSPL14/IPA1 and NF-YC8-12. 2. This complex regulates Wx and PDIL1-1.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Circadian regulation of storage substance accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates storage substances and eating quality through direct binding to key starch and protein genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-binding and functional studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCCA1, OsLHY, and Nhd1 form a circadian regulatory module. 2. They bind OsSSII-3, Wx, OsGluA2, and OsLHT1. 3. This module regulates rice storage substances and CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions with OsCCA1/Nhd1 to regulate storage substances and eating quality.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-binding and functional studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLHY participates in the OsCCA1/OsLHY/Nhd1 module. 2. The module targets key starch and protein genes affecting CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Storage substance regulation and NUE-linked protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in circadian and NUE-linked regulation of storage substances and grain protein content.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Regulatory studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nhd1 binds storage-related genes with OsCCA1/OsLHY. 2. It is also highlighted as a confirmed regulator of grain protein content.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression reduces protein and increases amylose with higher chalkiness; low expression maintains balance and enhances heat tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional expression analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QT12 encodes a Sec61 β subunit. 2. Its expression level affects protein/amylose balance and chalkiness. 3. Low expression improves balance and heat tolerance. 4. Upstream NF-Y complex participates in this temperature/storage regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Protein stabilization for starch synthesis and storage accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilizes OsGBSSI and OsPPDKB to promote starch synthesis; mutants reduce starch and protein accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant and interaction studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsHsp70-2 and OsHsp40-1 form a chaperone module. 2. This module stabilizes OsGBSSI and OsPPDKB. 3. Mutants reduce both starch and protein accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for stabilizing storage biosynthesis under stress or high-yield settings.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports stabilization of starch synthesis machinery and contributes to both starch and protein accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant and interaction studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsHsp40-1 cooperates with OsHsp70-2. 2. Their module stabilizes OsGBSSI and OsPPDKB to support storage accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates FGC3 and thereby affects cutin monomer synthesis and grain yield.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Regulatory association summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMADS6 can regulate FGC3. 2. Through FGC3 it affects cutin monomer synthesis and yield.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates FGC3 and thereby affects cutin monomer synthesis and grain yield.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Regulatory association summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMADS17 can regulate FGC3. 2. Through FGC3 it affects cutin monomer synthesis and yield.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes related to glycolysis and fatty acid synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulate genes for glycolysis and fatty acid synthesis, contributing to lipid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional regulation summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWR11a and OsWR11b upregulate glycolysis- and fatty-acid-synthesis-related genes. 2. They contribute to rice lipid metabolism relevant to CEQ.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts 2AP accumulation and agronomic traits by repressing BADH2.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional regulatory studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY19 negatively regulates BADH2. 2. This repression increases 2AP. 3. The gene is also reported to improve agronomic traits.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising aroma-improvement regulator if agronomic benefits are confirmed across backgrounds.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates FAVs; knockout increases FAVs without yield loss.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout and aroma phenotyping summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNAC021 negatively regulates fatty acid-derived volatiles via the lipoxygenase pathway. 2. Knockout increases FAVs without yield penalty.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No yield loss reported in the review, suggesting favorable deployment.
